title: Operating Room Block Reallocation
description: A hospital-operations and compliance workflow that enforces control checks while allocating operational resources and closing governance loops. Focus case: operating room block reallocation.
assumptions: Each reallocation case (T1) is managed independently and follows the same sequence of checks.
Operating room blocks (T2) and time slots (T3) listed as available in initial state reflect real operational availability.
Resource allocation predicates correspond to exclusive reservations (a resource reserved for one case at a time).
Approver roles (T15/T16) and credential reviewers (T9) are available and able to act when requested; their consumption models reflect single-use approvals in this workflow.
Facilities and supply checks are modeled as discrete verifications and may be repeated if resources change.
risks: Delays in credential or approver actions (T9/T15/T16) may block scheduling, causing throughput loss and potential patient safety impacts.
Incorrectly modeled exclusive reservations could allow double-booking of OR blocks or specialized equipment.
Missing or stale supply_kit (T12) or special_equipment (T13) availability can invalidate a confirmed schedule at short notice.
Insufficient linkage between operational readiness checks and final sign-off could produce formally closed cases that are not truly ready for procedure execution.
Overreliance on single-use approval_document consumption may not reflect real-world reissuance or revocation processes, risking compliance gaps.
